data.body:
[G4 child-consistency — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S6 1ba5: Docs — private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model); re-confirmation against the WHOLE EIGHT-child roster]

Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this docs child against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence/ADR 0002, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing). All 7 modes are CLEAN under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. EMPTY G4 findings list for this pairing.

Mode-by-mode:

1. Implicit shared state — CLEAN. Every value this child documents has a single named producer, and the producer→documenter chain is consistent: REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + the flock/portability seam (S1 6d56 AC4/What); disk-cap behavior + the watermark/janitor (S2 70c7 / S2b b73c); the descriptive credential error message+remedy (S1 6d56 AC5 raises it); ref/source + the origin/main default (S3 fa49 threads, S5 b7fa surfaces); the verified_at_sha record + in-toto Statement shape + HMAC limits (S4 30b3, whose What explicitly delegates the trust-model docs "limits documented in S6"). No documented value lacks a producer.

2. Conflicting assumptions — CLEAN. Every doc claim matches the behavior it describes: attested = pinned SHA / signs / records verified_at_sha (S4 30b3 AC1/AC2; S3 fa49 AC1); local = in-place checkout / never signs (S3 fa49 AC4 marks-unsigned, S4 30b3 AC4 bars-signing); "attested fails closed while local still runs" (S1 6d56 AC5); origin/main default (S3 fa49 AC2 / S5 b7fa AC1). No contradiction with any sibling.

3. Dependency gap — CLEAN at this child. This child is a pure documenter; each documented obligation has a named producing sibling that explicitly delegates the docs to S6: S1 6d56 ("see S6" for the credential message), S4 30b3 ("limits documented in S6" + in-toto shape), S5 b7fa ("Out: prose docs (S6)"), S2/S2b (disk-cap/watermark behavior). The EFS/NFS flock caveat + AWS placement is cross-ref'd to alto-fruit-punch per the parent's Out-of-scope. Checked the two plausible "documented-X-not-named-here" cases and both are benign, not high-confidence gaps:
   (a) Parent AC3's "source=local is the DOCUMENTED back-out that restores the prior in-place read behavior" — this child's AC3 documents the local-vs-attested + signs-vs-never-signs semantics but does not verbatim carry the "back-out/rollback" framing; however S3 (fa49) carries an explicit advisory AC "source=local is documented as the rollback to in-place reads" + its What back-compat bullet, so the back-out doc obligation has a named home and is not orphaned at the S6↔S3 seam.
   (b) Parent AC7's "free-space watermark / grace/age windows configurable with DOCUMENTED defaults (S2/S3)" — this child's AC5 documents "disk-cap behavior" + REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR but does not separately enumerate the S2b grace/max-age/reverify tunables; however S2b (b73c AC9) carries its own "Watermark, grace window, max-age, and reverify period are configurable with documented defaults" obligation, so the documented-defaults requirement has a home in the producing child. Under the benign-reading filter "disk-cap behavior" reasonably subsumes the watermark docs; no high-confidence consumer-impact gap.

4. Scope overlap — CLEAN. ref/source: this child owns the human PROSE (named, grep-able doc files); S5 owns the inline schema-text/documented-defaults (outputSchema/text) — each scoped out of the other (S5 Scope: "Out: ... prose docs (S6)"; this child Scope: "Out: code (S1-S5)"). in-toto Statement: S4 BUILDS the envelope, this child DOCUMENTS it (S4 What names the S6 hand-off). HMAC trust model: S4 produces the HMAC signing, this child documents its limits. Clean producer/documenter splits; no double-ownership of any one artifact. S6 is the only docs child; the ADR (parent AC8) is authored in S2/S4/S4b (code-side, "Out: code (S1-S5)" here), so no ADR-authoring overlap.

5. Ordering violation — CLEAN. The encoded dependency graph points the benign way: S4 (30b3) and S5 (b7fa) each carry a `blocks 1ba5` edge, so behaviors precede the docs that describe them (producer→documenter). This is coherent with the producer→consumer flow and asserts no contradiction or gap against this docs child. Parent AC9 only requires all-closed + green.

6. Consumer impact — CLEAN. This child is a terminal consumer/documenter; it produces no API/schema/env surface that a sibling depends on, so it imposes no downstream breakage. Its cross-refs (alto-fruit-punch, REB-289) are to out-of-epic trackers per the parent's Out-of-scope, not to siblings.

7. Residual references — CLEAN. All cross-refs resolve: docs/jira-sync-setup.md (this child's file_impact target, exists), README.md (exists; carries the MCP-setup block), REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR (S1), disk-cap (S2/S2b), the EFS/NFS flock caveat xref alto-fruit-punch (matches the parent's Out-of-scope note), the in-toto Statement (S4), and "Out: code (S1-S5)" (all five code siblings present in the roster).

Note (not a G4 finding on THIS pairing): the standing dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) / S4b (e308) — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check — is a code seam between S3 and S4/S4b; it does not touch this docs child. S6 documents the HMAC trust model + ref/source semantics regardless of how that code seam is resolved, introducing no new inconsistency.

Conclusion: no high-confidence cross-child inconsistency between this docs child and the parent/siblings. Every documented obligation maps to a named producing sibling (each of which delegates its docs to S6) or to an out-of-epic tracker per parent scope; the two enumeration nuances (source=local back-out framing; S2b watermark/grace-age tunable docs) are benign because the obligations are claimed by the producing children (S3 advisory AC; S2b AC9). EMPTY G4 findings list for this pairing.
